purpose: Dye sources, supply chains, and the canonical dye-process. Connects to apparel.json (garments take dye), professions.json (fullers + cactem keepers + alchemists), bestiary.json (sealies + cephalopods + sea snails as material sources), and faction economics (Bahiij owns the Cactem Strip; Fluxom historically dominated mollusca trade).

process.name: tunyan_canonical_dye
steps: soak materials in stale_urine + sea_salt + dye
process.fixative: stale urine + sea salt — historically accurate medieval-equivalent process. Fullers handle the soak step and are exposed to the urine (per professions.json fuller hazard cap).
process.rainy_season_modifier: weaving and dyeing peak in the rainy season because sun-drying can be done indoors via heat-of-living-spaces; outdoor sun-dry yards are unusable. Weavers and loomers are seasonal workers.

dyes.source_lore: An insect bred for dye. There is a strip of land — the Cactem Strip — designated for them; they feed on richly moist plants. Mix dye with salt.
supply_chain: cactem_keepers tend the colony at the Cactem Strip
alchemists in Bahiij refine the raw cactem into salt-mixed dye-paste
exported through Bahiij merchants; Sandrun Sanguire and Akeia are the largest customers
dyes.monopoly: Bahiij effectively monopolizes red-dye production. Other kingdoms have tried importing cactem; the insects do not survive outside the Strip's specific moist-plant ecosystem.

dyes.source_lore: The mollusca produce both white dye AND paper.
dyes.source_location: coastal kingdoms — Fluxom historically dominated; now also Cree and Sahm
supply_chain: soft tissue rendered for white pigment
single creature feeds two industries — paper for Asbir's archive + dye for ceremonial whites
dyes.monopoly: Pre-Fluxom-collapse, Fluxom controlled mollusca trade. Now contested between Cree and Sahm. Asbir is the primary buyer of mollusca paper.

dyes.source_location: temperate woodland regions — most kingdoms can grow it
arid + temperate regions — Sahm dominates mustard cultivation
supply_chain: seeds pressed and the residue dyed into a slurry
dyes.monopoly: Sahm dominates but does not monopolize

dyes.source_lore: Two-creature dye. Sea snails contribute the base; sealies' defensive sac contributes the deep tone. Sealie sac is harvested during sealie hunts.
dyes.source_location: coastal + open sea — Fluxom + Akeia + Bahiij + Sahm
supply_chain: sealie defensive sac extracted post-kill (extremely rare and valuable)
alchemists combine the two with the canonical dye process
sealie-derived purple is the deepest tone; snail-only purple is a paler variant
dyes.monopoly: no kingdom monopolizes the sea snail. Sealie-purple is monopolized by Fluxom and select Sahm + Aekon vessels — only sealie hunters can produce the deep tone, and sealie hunting is ~1% of vessels.

dyes.source_lore: Grass ground into paste, mixed with salt water; clothes dipped.
dyes.source_location: every kingdom can produce — the most accessible dye on Tunya
supply_chain: dye-paste prepared at the household level
dyes.monopoly: none. Green is the everyday color.

supply_chain: stigmas dried and ground to dye
expensive — saffron is labor-intensive to harvest
dyes.monopoly: Bahiij + Aekon split production; both export to other kingdoms

dyes.source_location: every kingdom — temperate to tropical nut groves

color_economy_implications.red_as_political_lever: Bahiij controls red. If Bahiij withholds red dye, Sandrun Sanguire's banners cannot be produced and Akeia consort robes cannot be replaced. This is leverage Bahiij has never used overtly. The threat is the leverage.
color_economy_implications.purple_as_status_marker: True deep purple requires a sealie kill. Wearing deep purple in court signals access to sealie-hunting vessels — itself a wealth signal. Akeia's queen mother and Dormas's high-quarter both wear it.
color_economy_implications.white_collapses_with_fluxom: Fluxom's collapse disrupted the mollusca trade. Asbir's archive paper supply has been rationed since Fluxom's fall; the Lord Curator hoards old paper as a strategic reserve.
color_economy_implications.fuller_class: Fullers handle the urine-soak. Their hazard cap is real. Some kingdoms (Dormas, Bahiij) have begun research into fixative alternatives — alchemy budgets are quietly increasing.
